title: Chocolate collar
subtitle: A tempered chocolate band applied around the outside of a cake
excerpt: A chocolate collar is a strip of tempered chocolate set on acetate, wrapped around a frosted cake, and peeled away to leave a smooth chocolate band. It requires tempered chocolate and a strip of acetate slightly taller than the cake. The method takes confidence; the results are professional.
sourceNotes: Chocolate work using acetate strips to create collars and curved decorations is documented in confectionery and patisserie manuals from the twentieth century. The principle of spreading tempered chocolate onto a surface, allowing it to reach the correct point of set, and wrapping it around a form before final setting is a standard professional technique adapted for domestic use. The tempering of dark chocolate by the tabling or seeding method is described in public-domain confectionery references, and the collar application is now widely reproduced in domestic baking guides.

content.text: The collar works because the chocolate is applied to the acetate just before it loses its flexibility. Too liquid and it runs; too set and it cracks when you wrap it. The window is about two minutes, so have the cake, turntable, and tape ready before you start spreading.
to the height of the cake plus 1 cm, and to the circumference of the cake plus 4 cm. Lay it shiny-side up on the work surface. Have the cake on a turntable at arm's reach.

Melt two-thirds of the {{dark-chocolate}} over a pan of barely simmering water to 55°C. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining chocolate in three additions, allowing each to melt. Stir until the temperature drops to 28 to 29°C, then return to the heat briefly to bring it back to 31 to 32°C. This is tempered: it will set with a snap and a gloss.

Spread the tempered chocolate evenly across the acetate strip with the icing spatula to about 3 mm thickness. Wait about 2 minutes: the surface should be losing its wet sheen at the edges but still moving slightly in the centre. Pick up the strip and wrap it around the cake with the chocolate against the frosting. The overlap holds it in place. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Peel the acetate away gently; the chocolate collar should lift free, leaving a glossy band on the cake.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 collar for a 20 cm round cake
